session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 9027 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the specified arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [195 lines omitted] ...
131→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 133→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 171→ "error": null 185→## Error Case 199→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 217→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by running the required commands to fetch and classify the PR feedback for PR #9027.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Fetch PR feedback and comments for PR 9027

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: TRUNK=$(erk exec detect-trunk-branch | jq -r '.trunk_branch') && git diff --stat -M -C "$TRUNK"...9027 2>&1 || echo "Git diff command completed"
Detect trunk branch and show file restructuring

session.tool_result:
{ "success": true, "pr_number": 9027, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9027", "pr_title": "Add objective link insertion to PR body assembly", "review_threads": [ { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5y4Vlf", "path": "src/erk/cli/commands/pr/shared.py", "line": 270, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Code Simplification**: Variable 'key_changes' declared but used only once - inline the string literal directly in .find() call: `idx = body.find("## Key Changes")`", "created_at": "2026-03-08T19:24:58Z" } ] } ], "discussion_comments": [ { "id": 4019816687, "author": "schrockn", "body": "Plan Queued for Implementation

Now I'll classify the PR feedback. Based on the fetched data, I have:
1. **1 Review Thread**: Line 270 in `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/shared.py` - Code simplification suggestion from github-actions bot 2. **6 Discussion Comments**: Plan submission metadata (2 comments), test coverage review (1 comment), dignified python review (1 comment), code simplifier review (1 comment), and tripwires review (1 comment)
Let me analyze and classify:
**Review Thread Classification:** - Thread ID: `PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5y4Vlf` - Author: `github-actions` (bot) - Issue: Variable `key_changes` declared but used only once - suggests inlining the string literal - Classification: `actionable` (bot style/refactoring suggestion) - Pre-existing: `false` (author is bot but this is a new addition in this PR, not file restructuring) - Complexity: `local` (single line change) - Action summary: Inline variable `key_changes` directly in `.find()` call
**Discussion Comments Classification:** - Comments 4019816687, 4019817279: Machine-generated erk metadata/workflow status (informational) - Comments 4019828843, 4019828974, 4019829111, 4019829200: Bot-generated review reports (test coverage, dignified python, code simplifier, tripwires) - all informational
